The company generously invests in training. Nokia reflects the Finnish way of life where work life balance is very strongly respected, employee benefits are solid including a 401 k match and generous PTO. I loved the work an...

- People with actual skill building and developing ideas are often used as the workhorses for people who are good at developing "paper architectures" and ideas. - TC is not competitive, and career growth is slow/non-existent due to saturation at the top. - Constant turnover in leadership, which has led to constant re-organization and layoffs. - Constant internal messaging about how the company is changing moral X or...

Career progression is not spelled out for you, so unless you are good at being creative/ have a lot of initiative you can become stagnant. Communication between business groups is lacking and can be very challenging. If you are uncomfortable with change, it can be very overwhelming. Adaptability is Key

evidence.role: Former employee - Member Of Technical Staff
evidence.text: Lack of management support, always trying to cut corners. Layoffs Company in Chaos

Management is chaotic, and often absent. Teams are broken up across the globe, making it easy for an employee to become lost between function groups. Co-Workers are often adversarial and berative, if not actually abusive, and there is nothing you can do about it (see also, absent management). Chaotic Management, Toxic culture
